entry.title: Re: Error in Scrap Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi,

Go to MM01 and extend material on sales organization D001 dist. chan.40 and plant D101.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Financial Accounting Certification Options - Please c...
entry.content:
Hi @Susankayla 

For more details on the C_TS4FI please access our SAP Certified Associate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Financial Accounting 

This certification verifies that you possess the core skills and proven skills in the area of SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting. This certificate is an entry-level qualification for Financial Accounting consultants on SAP S/4HANA, allowing them to understand and implement skills in projects and is relevant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition and SAP S/4HANA on-premise. It is an ideal starting point for a professional career.

 
For more details on the C_S4CFI please access our SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Financial Accounting  

This certification verifies that you possess the fundamental knowledge required to explain and execute core implementation project tasks to deploy, adopt and extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as well as core knowledge in Financial Accounting. It proves that the candidate has an overall understanding and in depth technical skills to participate as a member of a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation project team with a focus on Financial Accounting in a mentored role.
 
------------------
Please access our SAP Certifications site where you can browse though the list and there you have additional filter options.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Sales header text fields need to copy from quotation...
entry.content: Have you explored the option of Text Obligatory/not in config settings at VOTXN(Sales doc header>>in "text IDs in text procedure on the left side dialog structure)

entry.title: No active outbound service for appl. obj. 500000002620230001, outp. type MMIM_MATDOC_GDSMVMT_ESOA, B
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We face some error message "No active outbound service for appl. obj. 500000002620230001, outp. type MMIM_MATDOC_GDSMVMT_ESOA, BuPa ." when we want to see the PDF of goods issue for cost center. We already setup the output determination but still face the error.

Any solution ?

Thankyou!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Changing of Risk categories
entry.content:
"What is the risk if you do not run it, as we are using our own credit check, and the standard credit check is only setup for a warning, no block?"

It is self explanatory-No update means no revised credit limit updates for the risk category changed customers... is this acceptable-Please check.

Also what you mean by standard credit check--you mean static check in OVA8?

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi @Vitaliy-R ,

I was able to resolve the previous error.

But now I am getting the below error while inserting into the table.

@Cocquerel if you have some time, can you kindly help here ? I am pretty new to this Python world.

entry.title: Re: Sales header text fields need to copy from quotation...
entry.content:
Hi,

Have you checked copy control configuration and VOTXN text dermination settings?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
It seems you have added some additional partitioning in your SQL statement, so it is not getting row numbers for the whole table but for smaller partitions.

Could you restore it to

INNER JOIN 
  (SELECT 
     ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS "row_num", *
   FROM {source_table}
  ) AS "B"

and then re-run the cell?

entry.title: Re: Is there a standard S4/HANA api available for reading/creating / deleting SO10 Texts?
entry.content: They are generally available as part of any APIs associated with the business objects to which they are tied, but I don't believe there is a generic option for reading all types.

entry.title: Re: UI does not refresh on delete of instance through cus...
entry.content:
@Shahab2 

Can you try adding side effect annotations in the local annotation file? I think it works for S/4HANA 2022 as well. Please see the sections below in the document.

  • Side effect after executing an action
  • Refresh the navigation target

entry.title: Re: Changing of Risk categories
entry.content: On OVA8 we have dynamic, with only warning, as we have an ABAP program that does the credit check. Our credit limit field is 1.00, we use for the ABAP recommended credit limit field

entry.title: Re: What to start from to gain knowledge in FPSL?
entry.content:
Hi @LikhachevE 

The blog from our SAP expert Doris Lindner might be helpful to stay current with Live Sessions for Finance. 
Please have a look at our SAP S/4HANA for Financial Products Subledger  published on our SAP Help Portal. There you find  documentation materials and videos.
The blog SAP Financial product subledger overview might also be helpful.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: EBR (Log Buyoffs) in Process Industry in SAP Digital...
entry.content:
Hi,

Log Buyoff plugin is currently not supported in Order POD, see Plugins by POD Type.
EBR capabilities are planned in DM roadmap, so keep an eye on that for the future.
Running process orders in parallel in both Work Center and Order PODs is not supported, as each of theses PODs require very specific setup and works in different ways, see note #3469745 for more details.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP
entry.content:
@anil_lahori2 

Now that Managed BO with Unmanaged Save is available, I think there are hardly any use cases for a pure unmanaged scenario. You would use Managed BO with Unmanaged Save when you want to implement your own logic (e.g., using existing code or calling standard RAP BOs) for saving data to the database (or an external system), rather than having the RAP framework handle the saving.

entry.title: Re: Mass updation of Dep start date in Asset
entry.content:
Hi George

Is it allowed for S/4HANA Public Edition?

Regards

Clara.

entry.title: Re: Convert plan order to process order from MD04
entry.content:
Hi,

In OPPQ or OPPR, it seems that you have not defined the “Process order” type for the conversion. Retest by assigning the relevant order type. For OPPR also check whether you have assigned the correct MRP group to the material.

entry.title: Re: Convert plan order to process order from MD04
entry.content:
Hi,

if you convert a planned order into a process order the the Prod. Sched. Profile needs to be set to a PI related one.
I guess this is not set right now hence you get the message as PP01 is by standard used.
Set it to PI01 and run it again.

BTW: A order conversion of materials the are PPDS relevant should be performed from PPDS and not from S4.
PPDS is the scheduling leading system and the dates from that system needs to be taken over in the S4core system. In order to keep the ORDID a conversion from PPDS needs to be done. If you convert the order in S4 then a new order is used hence a not ORDID and that "can" lead to a rescheduling.

entry.title: Getting error 1935
entry.content: Getting the error while installation of 2008 crystal reports

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne July '24
entry.content:
Melbourne! we are back again for the 3rd time this year. Now in the thick of winter, the perfect time to get out and hang with your SAP buddies.

This time we are going to mix it up a bit and make it a Wednesday in case Thursdays don't work for your regular weekly commitments. The other bonus is that it is $28 Parma & Schooner night! but there are other good deals if you grab an early dinner too.

So far this year (in March & May) we have had a great mix of people at the Melbourne Stammtische, and alway great conversation. I have really enjoyed the relaxed and friendly interactions. It's been brilliant catching up with familiar faces, those I haven't seen for a long time and new ones.

We are now on par with meeting about every two months, with the idea to keep it consistent and provide ample opportunity for people to be able to make it, or just catch up more often. We are not too worried about getting big numbers, but more focused on good interactions. And on that front it has been succeeding, so I'm really looking forward to this next gathering. I hope to see you there, and be sure to spread the word around.

Wednesday 24th July 2024 at 5:30pm at The Bank on Collins 

 

 Ground floor/394 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000.

 

When you arrive, if you can't find us you can ask for a booking for the "SAP Regulars Table" 

Ben.

entry.title: Use TypeScript with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
entry.content:
Use TypeScript with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

When I started covering the SAP Cloud Application Model topic as a Developer Advocate, I had to start working with a programming language I had tried to avoid over the past years: JavaScript.
All my past development experience was around type-safe programming languages like Swift or GoLang. Type-safe means that the compiler does type-checking on your code. The compiler will validate types while compiling and throw an error if a type is assigned or used invalidly, like dividing a number by an array of Strings. Jumping into JavaScript made my head hurt quite a bit because it allowed me, at least most of the time, to do whatever I felt like doing. Only during runtime or within my unit tests did my code throw errors. Unfortunately, these errors also didn't help me understand what was happening.

const obj = { width: 10, height: 15 };


const area = obj.width * obj.heigth;
// This will result in NaN.

You can see where JavaScript comes from and what it was initially intended for - writing small, self-contained code scripts to complete smaller, concise tasks like handling a button press. It was created to make websites dynamic and enhance the user experience by providing advanced, interactive features. Over time, more and more functionality was added to the language because developers started to write more extensive code to achieve complicated tasks within the browser. JavaScript also evolved into a language that allowed for code to run on the server side with the Node.js flavor. Having server-side software run with a language that provides no compile-time type-safety felt like it would make code harder to maintain, understand, and develop.

I appreciate where JavaScript comes from and see its benefits, like its speed, its ability to be used in many use cases and environments, and its power to give the developer a lot of freedom. However, I am more comfortable having a safety net below me, so I jumped onto a JavaScript flavor, adding a static type-checker during design time—TypeScript.

What is TypeScript?

TypeScript provides a static type-checker around the JavaScript core. It helps make code more readable by allowing the definition of types in code and adding the possibility to define object types through interfacing and compile-time type checking. The beauty is that TypeScript does not alternate the underlying JavaScript code. Drop your working JavaScript code one-to-one into a TypeScript file, and your code will run and compile. TypeScript is not altering the JavaScript core; it is adding type safety. When you compile and build your code, TypeScript will remove your type declarations so that the compiled binary only includes pure JavaScript. That means you get all the creative possibilities and power JavaScript provides, but it allows you to add a safety net when doing your acrobatic endeavors.

let number = 5
let string = "hello"

number = string
// The compiler will warn you that "string is not assignable to type the number."

How do I get started?

Using TypeScript within your CAP projects is easy, and I will show you how!

  1. Install the TypeScript npm package for NodeJS.
npm i -g typescript ts-node
  1. Create a tsconfig.json file. Within that file, you can define JavaScript runtime environment dependencies and compiler options for TypeScript. e.g.:
{
    "extends": "@tsconfig/node12/tsconfig.json",
    "compilerOptions": {
      "preserveConstEnums": true
    },
    "include": ["src/**/*"],
    "exclude": ["**/*.spec.ts"]
  }

  1. Write your code in .ts files.

  2. Use the cds-ts command instead of cds to invoke TypeScript instead of plain JavaScript.

    The cds-ts command is being used to avoid precompiling TypeScript files to JavaScript on each execution; this will speed up the development process. For production, use cds to build the final binary!

CAP & TypeScript by example

If we use the Hello World example from the official CAP documentation, you would build a TypeScript CAP application like this:

  • Service Definition (world.cds):
service say @(path: '/say') {
  function hello (to:String) returns String;
}
  • TypeScript implementation (world.ts):
import { Request } from "@sap/cds"

module.exports = class say {
    hello(req: Request) {
        return `Hello ${req.data.to} from a TypeScript file!`
    }
}

The complete example can be found in the SAP-samples repository.

Conclusion

JavaScript is a powerful language widely used in web development, server-side programming, and many more places! It is an industry-standard, and millions of developers love it. However, it feels chaotic and gives way too much freedom to developers, so I decided to switch to TypeScript. TypeScript will provide the power of JavaScript but it'll help me better understand the code written and ensure that I don't do something silly. It is an excellent addition to programming with Node.js and CAP daily. 

I hope you enjoyed this little blog post about TypeScript, and I hope you will give it a try!

With that, keep Coding!

entry.title: Re: How to view deleted pricing condition records in SAP...
entry.content: Not sure on public cloud..Please try this program RV16ACHD in SE38 & check..

entry.title: Re: Getting error 1935
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions.

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2408
entry.content:
Overview

Providing a significant number of new features is an important goal of release upgrades to deliver innovations to our customers and partners. There are four major release upgrades per year following a predefined schedule as described in this blog post.

An upgrade is intended as a “no event” for customers and partners. For example, only compatible changes of released objects (APIs) will be delivered. We recommend performing a test of the custom-built productive apps in a test environment for the new release. The complexity of the technical dependencies between the custom-built apps and the underlying environment cannot be tested isolated, only including the ABAP environment on SAP-side. Therefore, we offer you to pre-upgrade selected systems 15 days in advance to test the applications prior to the planned standard-upgrade.

 

Purpose

This option enables a customer or partner to test the applications they have already developed on top of the new release a couple of days before the standard-upgrade. With SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk"), we follow the approach of keeping released objects stable and provide all enhancements in a compatible fashion. Early testing is an additional measure to ensure proper software quality and might be used to detect unforeseen regressions. However, this option is not intended to test new features or to consume new features earlier.

 

Approach

The standard-upgrade of all systems to release 2408 is planned for August 17/18.

A customer or partner can nominate one or more already existing systems for the pre-upgrade. A nominated system will be upgraded on August 2 within a planned downtime from 8:00 to 16:00 CET.

We recommend nominating a non-development system, such as a test system. The nomination of a development system is technically also possible, but fixes or other changes must not be imported into another system with a lower release. The nomination of a productive system doesn’t make sense because the purpose of the pre-upgrade is the re-test of the existing application before the productive system gets the new release.

According to the approach explained above, this offer is only relevant for customers or partners having at least two systems.

There might be situations where a correction of custom objects needs to be deployed. Such corrections are possible if the development / correction system is on the older release and is imported into the pre-upgraded system with the higher release.

Except potential fixes, the pre-upgrade is based on the same version as the standard-upgrade.

 

Costs

This offering is optional and free of any additional charges.

 

Planned New Features

The planned new features will be published on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment on July 19. This list gives you an overview of potential areas for re-testing. The purpose of the pre-upgrade is not to consume these features before the standard-upgrade, because the documentation on help.sap.com will be delivered later before the standard-upgrade. It would also not be possible to transport code of such an adoption, because subsequent systems are not yet on the new release during the pre-upgrade phase.

Independent of the upgrades of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we recommend developing automated tests for the custom-built apps to detect regressions early on and without manual effort for re-testing. Such automated tests are perfectly suited to be run in the pre-upgrade test phase.

 

Time Schedule

The following figure summarizes the general approach:

 

Milestones for release 2408:

  • July 5: Nomination of system(s) is possible in the Landscape Portal
  • July 19: Announcement of the planned new features
  • July 29: Pre-upgrade systems are known
  • August 2: Pre-upgrade
  • August 14: Potential decision to postpone the upgrade
  • August 17/18: Standard-upgrade

 

System Nomination

To nominate a system, please register the system(s) by July 29 (EOB) latest by using the Landscape Portal. You can find further information about Accessing the Landscape Portal and about Register Systems for Pre-Upgrade. Please do not create customer tickets for system nomination like in previous pre-upgrades.

With such a nomination, the customer agrees to an extraordinary planned maintenance on August 2 (see above).

 

Report an Issue

Issues should be reported as part of the regression test phase by August 9 (EOB) to allow for a sufficient time frame for issue analysis and potential corrections on SAP-side.

Please create a customer ticket by using component BC-CP-ABA.

If a detected issue is likely to endanger the productive use after the standard-upgrade, you should use ticket priority very high despite the fact that the pre-upgrade system is not a productive system.

 

Exceptional Process in Case of Unresolved Priority Very High Issues

In case of the aligned decision on Wednesday, August 14 (based on unresolved priority very high tickets) to postpone the upgrade, a dedicated mitigation process needs to be started together with the respective customer or partner, with the following boundary conditions:

  • Resolving the open priority very high issues remains the highest priority for both, SAP and the customer or partner, as it is also the prerequisite for the upgrade.
  • According to the estimated issue resolution, catching up the upgrade needs to be planned.
  • The upgrade leads to a business downtime of 6 – 8 hours.
  • The upgrade must be scheduled within 7 calendar days the latest after fixing all remaining open priority very high issues.
  • We will not provide any fixes for the old release after the original date of the standard-upgrade to the new release.

In case business-critical issues are detected after the upgrade:

  • Issues need to be reported as priority very high issues and will be processed accordingly.
  • Going back to the release before upgrade is not an option. Therefore, resolving the issues in the new release with the appropriate priority is the required approach.

 

Summary

We recommend performing regression tests in pre-upgraded test systems to avoid day-1 impacts of productive custom apps after the standard-upgrade.

entry.title: Re: Fast entry TAB in Prodution order
entry.content: Hi Dominik, Thank you for your reply. However, I cannot see the fast entry tab in the ECC system. Is it because it has been removed using SHDO, or does ECC not have a fast entry tab?

entry.title: VBScript deprecation plan
entry.content:
Hello,

VBScript will be debrecated in 2027. What is Power Designer plan about this debracation ? 

Do you have any opinion ? 

https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/windows-it-pro-blog/vbscript-deprecation-timelines-and-next-steps/ba-p/4148301

entry.title: SAC story pie chart does not show data labels/ legend if only one value
entry.content:
I have a SAC story with some pie charts. When the data is grouped into multiple groups, I see the data labels/ legends so that it is clear what the colors represent. But if I have all values in the same group, the data labels/ legends are not showing. Therefore it is not clear what the figure represents. Is it good or bad...?

How can I force the display of data labels/ legends?

I cannot find an option on the widget for this.

Please see the two screen shots.

Legend okLegend not ok

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
A greate blog, and we tried to implement for multiple scenarios and seems working.

But when we tried to utilise the same for file to file scenarios it is failing as receiver determination steps, with same data in partner directory it failed with error "net.sf.saxon.trans.UncheckedXPathException: Error reported by XML parser processing null", but for same setup we placed XML and tried to run it seems to be working.

Also, in the trace it in not showing as failed just stopped after the xslt operation 

Any idea how it is behaving, or any suggestion that help us for file to file scenario via pipeline

entry.title: Re: Error in Scrap Sales Order

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere OData Consumption API for Analytic Model
entry.content:
Hi CasCriel,

the documentation is quite specific, in that only authorization_code type flow is supported with Datasphere 0AUTH. (see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/9f804b8efa8043539289f42f372c4862/3f92b46fe0314e8ba60720e409c219fc.html)
Hope that helps.
Best Regards,

Irvin.

entry.title: Re: Delete user in FSM
entry.content: Hi Guido, Select the user ID you want to delete, the delete tab will activate above (will change its colour to blue) and then please press the delete tab. Please take care of select the particular ID only that you want to delete.

entry.title: Re: Consume an Event using Event Consumption Model
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for the reply and helping here.  Local event consumption is not of help for my scenario.

Local consumption of business events are possible only for C1 released RAP BO interface.  In my case, I need to do some follow on activities on Outbound delivery creation (and change).  With no BADIs provided for deliveries, the only way to do this is using events.  The Outbound delivery events are non-RAP events and I cannot consume using Local consumption.  

I am trying to see if I can consume the events which are not C1 released RAP BO interface, without using BTP Event mesh.

 

Thanks,

Vijay

entry.title: SAP script error checking
entry.content:
Hi,

When a command is send to SAP like "SAPsession.FindById("wnd[0]").resizeWorkingPane(170, 25, False)" and an error occurs I'd like to log the error and let the script continue after user input (if needed) to fix the error. Next I want to use the Log to examine the errors and fix them.

I was thinking to do this via an additional sub that handles the sap command and catches the error like this:

****************************************************************

Private sub resize() 'SAPCommand to be executed

SAPCommand(SAPsession.FindById("wnd[0]").resizeWorkingPane(170, 25, False))
SAPCommand(SAPsession.FindById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nsu3")
SAPCommand(SAPsession.FindById("wnd[0]").sendVKey(0))

End Sub

 

Private sub SAPCommand(input)         '//SAPCommand to be executed

try
    Run SAPCommand
Catch ex as Exception
    Log("Error for: " & input & vbCRLF & ex.tostring)
    msgbox("Fix the error for command: " & input)
end try

end sub

****************************************************************

I know the above code does not workout at all but it's just for illustration of what I'd like to accomplish.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: EBR (Log Buyoffs) in Process Industry in SAP Digital...
entry.content:
Hi @ManoelCosta

Thanks for an update. We will keep an eye about this update on DM Roadmap.

 

Regards,
Kartik

entry.title: Re: Delete user in FSM
entry.content: Yes, but the user will not disappear for the list of user.

entry.title: Re: How to find a table or FM for field details in module...
entry.content:
Look at the dynpros of the program, D020S, and use a statement such as IMPORT DYNPRO or, as it's an internal statemet, better call a wrapper FM such as RPY_DYNPRO_READ.

NB: D0[21-23]S are structures and not database table, what did you actually try?

entry.title: Re: Personal migration journey BTP: from Workflow Management to Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello @smarchesini ,

If you use bus. rules, just for data, which you can define on dev (where edit version is), then it is ok. But in WFM I used to use it for rule, where I defined system level where the process is (dev, test or prod). So on test, I need to put there string "test". I use it eg. for adding some additional watermark texts into mail notifications - like "This is TEST appravl only!" - I`m not able to find other way how to decide from a process, on which tenant (subaccount) it is run. Of course it can be some external service, like feature flags etc..., But thats not good - to add other dependency.

But recently I found in a roadmap, that decision tables should be modifiable.

Neverthless, because of lack of other options, I made a workaround - I created secodn process with one decision. I export/import the editable version of that process into every tenant (dev, test, prod), so I can change it directly on each system. And after deployment, you can call that decision by api. This serves its purpose, but not as elegant, as it should/would be when in process itself.

entry.title: Custom Field and Logic - Publish failed
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

Greetings!!!

We are on OnPrem and have been using the Custom Field and Logic for Business Context Purchase Order Item as Code List Based on CDS View. I have been adding the curtain annotations like @ObjectModel and Consumption. 

Problem - It is not generated properly and not allowing to publish. 

Any idea who we can do from Backend, I have seen couple of Tcode for this but it is associated with TR. 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Bharani

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hello, I am from India, currently living in Switzerland. I will be writing blogs by sharing my experience in SAP Controlling.

entry.title: En empleados con tramos no se calcula bien la base de cotización en el tramo que supera el tope máx.
entry.content:
Hola, buen día,


Por la naturaleza de la actividad de la empresa, solemos tener muchos empleados cuya nómina mensual está dividida por tramos y hemos detectado que en empleados topados, al llegar al tramo donde se supera la base de cotización de este año en España, SAP no ajusta bien la base de cotización de modo que, agregando las bases de cotización calculadas en tramos anteriores a la base de este último tramo donde se supera la base máxima de cotización se llegue a dicha base máxima. Lo que se calcula en ese último tramo es inferior a lo que debería salir para que la suma de todas las bases calculadas del mes alcancen el total de la base máxima de cotización.
 
Un cordial saludo. Muchas gracias.
Alberto Carpintero

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP (Bangalore, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP-based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment.

Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday 10th July 2024 in Bengaluru, India from 9:00 - 16:00 IST. 

Location: Deloitte India, Floor 18, Prestige Towers, Bangalore Branch & ATM, 99/100, Residency Rd, Shanthala Nagar, Ashok Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560025

The event is kindly being hosted by Deloitte India, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page.

The instructors are Developer Advocates Sheena M K , Ajay Soreng and Shilpa Shankar. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Connector License Refresh
entry.content:
Hi Sujit,

You don't refresh a license with that button. You refresh the certificate that allows to establish the subaccount connection to BTP. 

Best regards,
Markus

entry.title: Re: Inputs for CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2 - BOM explosion report - CS11
entry.content:
These are my inputs for the FM  CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2

AUMGB = "X" , CAPID = "PP01", DATUV = "MAST-ANDAT",

MKTLS = "X", MEHRS = "1", MTNRV = "MAST-MATNR",

STLAL = "01", STLAN = "1", STPST = "0", SVWVO = "X",

WERKS = "MAST-WERKS", and VRSVO = "X".

After getting executed, when data come in STB table, I want to fetch the following fields from it.

STB-IDNRK (Components), STB-OJTXP (Components Description),

STB-MTAR (Component Type), STB-MENGE (Component Quantity), STB-MEI (Base Unit of Measure).

Could you please help me with it?

entry.title: Distribution unit is STD in cproject instead of Hours
entry.content:
Hello, 

I created an item and then saved the project by selecting a project template. 

The distribution unit in the project template is Hours

When i create the project in reference to an item and select this project template, the unit is STD stunde in german which mean hours. 

Please, how can i change this to Hours ? 

I tried to change the unit in override default setting but it does not work 

BR.

entry.title: Re: Reference Document is B in KE24
entry.content:
Dear Sridhar, 

I hope the following items may help you:

2054445 - Reference item number is not populated consistently in COPA document

2718989 - FAQ in CO-PA (Part VI.)

Best Regards,

 

Gabriel Coleti

entry.title: Broadcasting SAP BW Analysis for Office report
entry.content: I am looking to broadcast SAP BW Analysis for Office report, Is it possible? If yes, please guide.

entry.title: ODataModel not getting called when adding authorization header
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a weird problem that suddently appeared. We have an fiori application where we call a OData secured by a JWT Bearer token. We added the token when creating the odata model.

 

                    const oDataModel = new ODataModel({
                        "serviceUrl": "/odata/v2",
                        "headers": {
                            "Authorization": "Bearer " + accessToken.AccessKey
                        }
                    });

 

This has worked for us up until last Monday, when it suddenly stopped working from within Business Application Studio.

In WebDeveloperTools I can see that the response is a 401, that takes much shorter time than expected from the API

I cannot see the call in the Terminal

 

info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$metadata?sap-language=EN
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService2>/$metadata?sap-language=EN
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch

 

 

If I however comment out the Authorization header in my call, or even replace the header name with anything else it behaves as expected. I get a popup asking for Basic Auth, and I can see the call in the terminal.

 

info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$metadata?sap-language=EN
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$metadata?sap-language=EN
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch
info backend-proxy-middleware /odata/v2/$metadata         <-------------
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/
info backend-proxy-middleware /sap/opu/odata/sap/<OtherService>/$batch

 

 

I don't understand what is happening, but to me it seems like either Chrome or backend-proxy-middleware suddenly filters out calls with an Authorization header.  It seems like it's working on our version deployed on a Gateway Hub, so I tend to think it's the backend-proxy-middleware that does something.

Regards
Mattias

entry.title: Re: Draft Conversion Triggers Duplicate Approval in SAP B...
entry.content:
Hi staseeb,

I would ask you to check the issue in the latest patch as there are several known issues in regards to the topic in question.

Refer to SAP Note 3428615 and 3411835 for the same.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: Modeling an advanced Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @STE ,

Thank you for your insights.

My question was that if we define a single hierarchy in Datasphere with Hierarchy with Directory method and create a variable on the dimension where the hierarchy is defined, how do we get the hierarchy structure of the dimension in the variable?  Any ideas on how to show a hierarchy structure in the variable as well?

I am getting a flat structure with this method. It shows a hierarchy structure in the table.

Thank you.

entry.title: No need to reinvent the wheel to improve citizen service.
entry.content:
I like the term the citizen consumer.  Each one of us is a citizen and also a consumer.  We deal with governments at multiple levels (local, state, federal) and we deal with companies as consumers / customers.  Commercial companies are in fierce competition for our money, so they need to provide excellent services and experiences to win us as customers.  We, as citizens, want the same service that we get as consumers when we deal with our government.

Governments know this and are acting. The US Federal Government has passed an executive order to transform customer experience and service delivery while the German government has passed the Online Access Act  (Onlinezugangsgesetz, OZG).

Governments are very different from commercial companies in many ways and have many unique processes and requirements. Despite this, governments can still use commercial software for many processes, often with few  modifications or customizations.

A good example is the SAP Endorsed App, LIVE CONTRACT Public by SYNCPILOT. 

LIVE CONTRACT Public by SYNCPILOT is a platform for governments to provide online digital services to their citizens. It will enable ad-hoc meetings as well as provide online appointment scheduling where citizens are assigned to an available agent using skill matching to best fulfill the citizen needs. 

During the online meeting , the government worker and citizen can interact barrier-free and share information through presentations, co-browsing, screensharing, whiteboards, videos, voice, etc.  They can even complete and legally sign documents / forms electronically and securely together in real time - without changing media.  The use of voice and video is done entirely in LIVE CONTRACT without the need for any additional 3rd party software / systems.​ LIVE CONTRACT will enable governments to digitalize and improve service with minimal effort as it was designed to seamlessly integrate into existing back-office systems and processes. It can be deployed rapidly without the need to replace existing back-office systems.

Built on top of the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) LIVE CONTRACT Public by SYNCPILOT is a SaaS offering that supports strong data privacy policies and is GDPR compliant. SYNCPILOT is the winner of the SAP Global SAP Customer Experience Partner Excellence Award 2022 for SAP Business Technology Platform Customer Success.

The City of Heidelberg is already using LIVE CONTRACT as “Virtuelles Bürgeramt” to improve the service for its citizens. –You can find a 2-minute video here showcasing its use. It is in German language but you can switch on closed captions and auto translate to any language.

More details can be found on the SAP Store listing and on the SYNCPILOT site.

Please send me a message here or reply with comment below for more information and to discuss further.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content: There is something missing in the INSERT statement. Look at previous week.

entry.title: Re: Auto XLD , user mapping between different user Ids
entry.content:
Hello SaikatMukherjee, 

The target TR will be created with the user running the XLD distribution in the change document, this is the design behavior. So, it is expected that this user is existing with same SU01 user in the system where the TR is going to be created.

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: Scheduling agreement has wrong status
entry.content: On trying "Withdraw from approval" option, the status of scheduling agreement automatically gets set to "Rejected", how can we process in this case?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Backend App - Entity - List Field Type
entry.content: Thanks, I tried that and that worked.  I was hoping the list type was like a Sharepoint List pre-defined "Choice" type, but it is just a "List" of values, unless I'm missing something or is there a different way to restrict what values users can populate in an entity field value?

entry.title: Re: Can I block every Supplier Invoice in S/4Hana Cloud
entry.content: Hi Raphael, yes we found the solution to run a validation so that the Payment Block R is set in the Journal entry. In the BADI FIN_ACDOC_VALIDATION. In addition we added Paymentblock 'R' to all our payment terms so that they are filled automatically.

entry.title: Re: SAP Hana Running Hyper-v Windows Server 2019 or 2022
entry.content: Hi kishan , Thanks for your assistance. I will check the missing permission in my profile. I found some more information on the SAP website, but I still think it is outdated, especially that the reference is to Windows Server 2012R2 or 2016, there is a URL stating that hyper-v does not run Linux in the onpremise environment. https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/virtualization/3362185750.html Linux in Virtual Environments: Linux not supported with Hyper-V on-premises, but supported with Azure https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows- server/virtualization/hyper-v/supported-suse-virtual-machines-on-hyper-v I'll leave the URLs to centralize what I'll be reading and feeding into this post. SAP Systems on Windows Server 2019 and 2016 https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2751450 Check Cluster Hyper-v https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2624843 https://me.sap.com/notes/2419847 https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/virtualization/3362185785.html https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/virtualization/3362185806.html My understanding so far is that SAP is compatible with hyper-v, using Suse or redhat as a guest, but I haven't checked with SAP HANA yet. Regards. Darley Campos

entry.title: No data available for memo record types
entry.content:
In the App Manage Memo Records 2.0 and App Import Memo Records 2.0 the selection for "Memo Record Types" is giving error message "No Data Available".


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No data available for memo record types
entry.content:
Hi,

Kindly, refer to the solution given in SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3485733

 

Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Edit Component Text in the UI Canvas 📝
entry.content:
Just released! You can edit a component's text attributes from within the UI canvas, without going to the Properties pane.

This seems to be true of any component with a text type property, like titles, text, and cards.

The text seems to be editable in the canvas if the binding type is static text or a simple text formula without any functions. Otherwise, you have to manually change the binding type to static text.

entry.title: Problem: Multiple Freight Settlement creation in SAP TM
entry.content:
For 1 freight order the system creates automatically different freight settlements to different dates automatically and that is not correct.

It should be that for 1 Freight order the system creates only 1 freight settlement document.

The steps are following in the system:

When departed it makes automatic goods issue of the delivery note.

Furthermore 1 Freight settlement is automatically created.

When the GI is registered then 1 correct freight settlement is automatically created (via JOBPLANER)

This example was correct.

But suddenly to a later point for example 03.01.2024 or 28.12.2023 a further freight settlement is automatically created. And that is not correct.

The yellow marked freight settlement documents were created automatically without any change in the system.

Here are some Screenshots:

 

I checked already the customizing and the master data I did not find an issue in these areas.

Is there maybe an SAP Note? Or is it there anything in Coding ABAP?

How is it possible to solve this problem?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Backend App - Entity - List Field Type
entry.content: I'm not aware of a special feature for this. You have to check on the client side.

entry.title: Where can I start learning the deployment options available for SAP Master Data Governance?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring the SAP Master Data Governance Deployments learning journey

Overview
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to evaluate common business scenarios and suggest the SAP Master Data Governance deployment option that should be applied for each scenario.

Learning objectives
In this learning journey, you will explore the business relevance of master data management and the different deployment options available for SAP Master Data Governance.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge


Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you - We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Linking Calendar Hierarchy and Fiscal Hierarchy Date...
entry.content:
@pooja_elangovan01 

The LINK function might not work if the date dimension in both model are of different hierarchy types. If its a user defined date dimension then in Fiscal date dimension, you can try maintaining an attribute with fiscal to calendar mapping. So maintain the calendar months against the calendar periods. Then try setting up the LINK as below

 

Date([d/Measures] = "SUM" = LINK [OPEX], [d/Measures] = "SUM", [d/Date] = [d/Date].[p/calendarperiod])

calendarperiod is the attribute. 

Hopefully this may work.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAC story pie chart does not show data labels/ legend...
entry.content:
@Chr_Frier 

Try following the below note:

3420049 - Pie Chart is not showing legend for single dimension in SAP Analytics cloud

Excerpts from Note:

Reproducing the Issue

  1. Log in to SAC.
  2. Open the story with a Pie chart.

Notice that the legend is not visible in the pie chart.

Cause

Legend is not enabled for the single dimension.

Resolution

Follow the below steps to see the legend :

  1. In the Pie chart click on 3 dots.
  2. Click on Show and hide.
  3. Enable the legend option.
  4. Save the story.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: System Email Notification Status Report
entry.content:
Hi @Haridha_P,

Thank you for this great article on how to build a Story Report on email notifications. I have granted the necessary permissions in RBP as stated in the article, but I don't have Email Notifications as an available data source. What am I missing in order to build this report?

Best regards,

Monica

entry.title: Re: Bring SAP Ariba data into SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Thanks Ramesh for the feedback!

1. We do not have a detailed video for the mission that you are trying to implement.

2. Yes HANA Cloud can be used with adjustments for extractor application's interaction with HANA. We now have Ariba extractor application available as business content for customers, and this uses HANA Cloud. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/6eb1eff34e4c4b1f90adfbfba1334240/ac708c6a9c0349ad8231eca616550801.html

entry.title: Getting the error while installation of 2008 crystal reports
entry.content: Error 1935

entry.title: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 21 - Maintenance Logistics with SAP EWM
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog post for the SAP EWM Podcast Episode 21 – Maintenance Logistics with SAP EWM.

Maintenance is a topic that is basically closely linked to the warehouse topic: be it the forklifts, the components of the conveyor systems or other equipment that is installed in the warehouse – everything has to be maintained somehow at some point in time. And of course, there are also specific warehouses, which store the components for the maintenance of machines in production or any other type of asset – and for these warehouses a warehouse management solution is mandatory.

For this use case, it is important that Warehouse processes integrate directly with Maintenance processes, to ensure stock transparency or to enable direct picking of spare parts for maintenance orders.

A modern and intuitive warehouse management solution like SAP EWM helps those warehouses, to ensure error-free work, to automate processes and to gain 100% transparency and traceability.

SAP Plant Maintenance and SAP Enterprise Asset Management

If we talk about maintenance logistics with SAP EWM  another SAP module plays an important role. SAP Plant Maintenance (SAP PM) which is part of the umbrella solution SAP Enterprise Asset Management (SAP EAM).  

SAP Enterprise Asset Management helps customers to achieve maximum asset availability with the longest possible life of the asset. This means reducing the downtime of assets and resources while avoiding costly spare parts procurement. It provides tools for managing and structuring technical objects such as assets, assemblies, equipment and so on.

And SAP EAM also supports customers in maintenance and repair. These can be planned or recurring inspections or it could be more sophisticated strategies like predictive maintenance.

In addition, SAP EAM also takes care of the execution which is usually done with the so-called maintenance order. This is a request to perform a maintenance task on a maintenance object on a specific date. In addition, the maintenance order is a tool for the documentation of maintenance tasks. The order is used to

  • plan specific maintenance
  • monitor the implementation of maintenance and
  • record and account for the costs incurred as a result of maintenance

The data of the order is included in the maintenance history and is therefore of the utmost importance for evaluations and future planning of such measures.

Integration points between SAP EWM and SAP EAM

With SAP EWM, companies can optimize warehouse logistics for service parts and maintenance components. 

Improved Stock Management
It is important especially for unplanned and urgent maintenance to have 100% stock transparency and quick access to the spare parts. In addition, maintenance often also stores expensive spare parts, which are sometimes also managed by serial numbers.

With SAP EWM, customers can manage stocks in a storage bin-specific, batch-specific, serial-number-specific, and also load carrier-specific. In particular, the batches and serial numbers are then also recorded exactly in the context of traceability during repair or replacement.

100% Stock Transparency with SAP EWM

Improved Picking and Staging
As mentioned above, maintenance orders are created in SAP Enterprise Asset Management for planned or unplanned maintenance. These orders also contain the components and spare parts required for the process, which are stored in an EWM-managed warehouse. These components can be entered manually in the order or they can be determined via a task list.

The maintenance order automatically triggers a reservation for its components, for which a staging must take place. The result of the staging, if it is an EWM-managed storage location, is then an outbound delivery that is transferred to EWM.

Special document types can then be found in EWM for these outbound deliveries - technically, this is solved via a special initiator process code for maintenance, which is transferred from the ERP to EWM via the standard interface. 

This makes it easy to configure special processes for picking and staging in the warehouse for maintenance outbound deliveries. And this is important because when we talk about the picking and staging of maintenance components, various processes are conceivable.

  • Pick-up by a service technician directly in the warehouse
  • Staging of the components to a specific asset or machine to be maintained or to be moved to a work center in a specific building
  • Shipping of the components to an external address 

Staging of Maintenance Components with SAP EWM

The object "Recipient Location"
To better organize these processes, a new object – the recipient location – has been defined in the integration between EAM and EWM. It is defined at plant level and can be assigned to each maintenance component and it is comparable to the production supply area from the production supply process.

SAP EWM uses the recipient location to determine a specific staging area where, for example, the service technician can pick up the components. Therefore the following steps are required: 

  1. Define possible recipient locations as master data in the system
  2. Assign recipient locations to the maintenance order items
  3. Define a staging area determination in SAP EWM based on the recipient location for the outbound delivery items

Recipient Location

The actual staging and therefore the creation of the outbound delivery can take place, for example, using transaction MB26 or MIGO  - automatically in background or manual.

It is not necessary to specify batches or serial numbers. The selection can be made by logistics within the SAP EWM stock removal strategy or during picking. In addition, the transaction checks whether outbound deliveries have already been created and prevents a multiple staging for the same maintenance order.

In SAP EWM features like wave management, picking order  creation, packing, and staging can then be used without restrictions to optimize the warehouse processes.

With the goods issue posting, the consumption posting of the components to the maintenance order takes place.

SAP PM-EWM Integration - Document Flow for Staging

 

Demo Video of SAP EWM - SAP PM Integration
The following short video shows the seamless integration for componenten staging of a SAP PM maintenance order with SAP EWM. 

Video "SAP PM-EWM Integration"

Customer Stock
Another EWM function that is of interest for plant maintenance, is the so-called "customer stock". This is stock is handed over from a customer to a service provider to perform maintenance. The stock is then physically located at the service provider and can be processed there in an EWM-managed warehouse, but the stock is of course non-valuated because it is still owned by the customer.

A typical example could be an aircraft or turbine that is temporarily handed over to a service provider for maintenance. Technically, this has been possible with EWM since Release S/4HANA 2022 . Technically, stock usage B is used for this and the customer is the owner of the stock.

 Customer Stock with SAP EWM

Customer Stock in the SAP EWM Warehouse Monitor

You can find more information around the topic of maintenance logistics with SAP EWM here: 

Plant Maintenance Supply with SAP EWM (product documentation)
Serial Number Management with SAP EWM (product documentation)

entry.title: Re: Broadcasting SAP BW Analysis for Office report
entry.content:
Hi there,

Yes, you can broadcast SAP BW Analysis for Office reports. Here's a quick guide:

 

  1. Create Your Report: Develop it in SAP Analysis for Office with the required data and visualizations.
  2. Set Up Broadcasting: Use SAP BW to schedule and broadcast the report.
  3. Define Recipients: Choose recipients and delivery methods (email, SAP Business Workplace, etc.).
  4. Schedule and Activate: Set intervals (daily, weekly) and activate the broadcast.

 

For specifics, consult SAP documentation or your SAP administrator.

entry.title: Get unitary actual cost of MLCCSPD report
entry.content:
Dear colleagues.

We are searching in MLDOC and MLDOCCCS tables to get the actual cost with component split of a material but it's not posible to restrict values and get the same actual cost than in MLCCSPD transaction.

This material does not have stock because all the produced quantitiy is in WIP and consumed (movs 261/262) in production orders during months.

It seems that there's no way to get the actual cost with cost component split for a mont/year/kalnr if there is no stock.

Do you know any function module of the needed filters to get the amount for the cost lot size?

Thanks in advance and best regards,

entry.title: Re: Condition Contracts settled as a service Invoice from Customer
entry.content: Hi Harry, we have the similar requirement, how did you achieve this requirement? thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 21 - Maintenance Logistics with SAP EWM
entry.content: You can also listen to the podcast episode in English and in German on Spotify, Amazon Music, Apple Podcasts or Sway! Enjoy!

entry.title: ValueHelp showing [Object Promise] instead of value
entry.content:
Hi !

I have problem with additionalBinding definition of valueHelp:

 

 

: { lineItem: [{ position: 29 }],
  identification: [{ position: 29 }] }
  .textArrangement: #TEXT_LAST
  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { name: 'zqm_claims_code_gr_head',

                                          element: 'qcodegrp' }
                                          }]

  qmgrp;
  : { lineItem: [{ position: 39 }],
  identification: [{ position: 39 }] }
  .textArrangement: #TEXT_LAST
  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [ { entity: { name: 'zqm_claims_code_head',                                                                     
                                            element: 'code' },
  additionalBinding: [{ localElement: 'qmgrp', element: 'qcodegrp', usage: #RESULT }
  ]}]
  @Consumption.filter.selectionType: #SINGLE
  @Consumption.filter.multipleSelections: false
  qmcod;

 

Error im getting is :
Error creating contexts for 

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zqm_claims_srv/srvd_f4/sap/zqm_claims_code_gr_head/0001;ps=%27srvd-zqm_claims_srv-0001%27;va=%27com.sap.gateway.srvd.zqm_claims_srv.v0001.et-zqm_claims_qmel.qmgrp%27/zqm_claims_code_gr_head with start index 0 and length 2 - Error: Unsupported type: com.sap.gateway.srvd_f4.zqm_claims_code_gr_head.v0001.zqm_claims_code_gr_headType

    at Object.formatLiteral ( ... }

any hints on how i can fix the issue ?

entry.title: 2LIS_04_P_MATNR - Basic Finish Date not updating as expected
entry.content:
We are trying to understand why, when extracting data using 2LIS_04_P_MATNR, the Basic Finish Date values extracted are not always the same as in the transaction CO03 or table AFKO?

In transaction OPL5 Release versions for order types have been configured, and I've seen that in some help documentation there are mentions that if data from AFKO is needed that the Release Versions should not be used.

Any assistance in understanding this would be appreciated.

SAP BW/4HANA

entry.title: SAPUI5 ui.table with SmartField on JSONModel
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have an interesting requirement. I want to create a table where my users can add new records. For this I use a sap.ui.table with input fields. This table has a binding with a JSONModel to store the new records. My oData Service has Annotations to generate value helps so I would like to add SmartFields in my table so I can re-use these value helps. So there are 2 options I think:

1. Can I use regular input fields in my sap.ui.table with generated value helps from my annotations? 

2. Can I put SmartFields in my table that uses a JSONModel to "write" my values but uses oData to retrieve valuehelp annotations? 

Thanks!

 

 

 

Best regards,

Axel Dion

entry.title: Email Notification when candidates are added to a Talent Pool based on a Saved Search
entry.content: Is there an option to setup email notifications when the candidates are being added to a Talent Pool based on SavedSearches? e.g. we have 4 talent pools for 4 different locations. We want that if a candidate is added to the Location1 talent pool, the admin(s) of the Location1 is notified and so on for the other location.

entry.title: Re: POWER BI Resource Usage
entry.content: I notice this issue occurs when I utilize the table function; otherwise, only one DAI is used in SM50.

entry.title: Re: EWM-WCU IDOC /SCWM/WMTOID01 seems not providing synta...
entry.content:
possible solution

  1. use /SCWM/WMTORD standard, allowing the external WMS to create a open product warehouse task
  2. use 711/712 like WPT
  3. Source + destination storage bin is the actual storage bin for the stock and can be equal
  4. exclude 711/712 like WPT in external WMS interface to avoid outgoing /SCWM/WMTORD for created WT
  5. use BAdI at end of WT creation CR_POST to start confirmation of incomming difference WT
    This confirmation can use a DIFW like exception and always quantity zero

By this, external WMS can sent stock adjustments, which are immediately populated to /SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER

entry.title: Re: Rough goods receipt using SAP EWM
entry.content:
It depends why you need that... You may use the Transportation Unit process and Unloading function if you want more detailed truck evidence. If you dont want to, you may use just unload or pack before GR. Also if you want to just check the goods you may:

- create inbound delivery based on Delivery note

- check the goods based on delivery note

- adjust inbound delivery based on result from previous step, pack and GR

There are many additional possibilities like POSC / QM with counting etc

 

Standa

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Intent based navigation - open target app in p...
entry.content: What do you propose?

entry.title: Re: Can I adjust the number of digits displayed after dec...
entry.content: @Yilin_Z thank you for letting me know!

entry.title: Re: SAC story pie chart does not show data labels/ legend...
entry.content:
Thank you so much for the quick reply, @N1kh1l

'Legend' was actually chosen, but at your indirect request, I turned it off first and then on again. So now it works despite the net change being zero.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - Import Validation Settings
entry.content: Thank you very much for your feedback. From your response, I gather that there is no other option but to create the validation rules manually. Is there nevertheless an explanation for the Excel?

entry.title: Re: All you need to know about Electronic Bank Statement - CAMT 054 format
entry.content:
Hello Ratul,

Very useful and helpful detailed content on how CAMT054 works. I have only one basic question (though you have talked about it in your blog) that why <54> needs to be concatenated to interpret VGEXT as could be seen in its standard program also:
 

As because of this only External Transaction Type needs to be maintained with <54> hence:

So, this is absolutely ok to maintain the External Transaction Type entries as above (though I could see same way in your write-up too).

Still if you can help me in understanding once more please.

Best Regards,
Minakshi Karan

entry.title: Re: Enter is not working in F4.
entry.content: After entering some search values in F4 Dialog, Click on search button is working. But entering some search values and click on "Enter" from keyboard is not working. how i can achieve this. Please help me.

entry.title: AppCreator: Validate business configuration of service Mobile Connectivity failed - Error
entry.content:
Hello,

currently we have some problems on customer side while creating the SSAM2310 App from AppCreator-Program /MERP/CORE_CPMS_APPCREATE_PROG.

We are using TCI-Notes and have implemented several notes for AppCreator also.

The Error says:

"Update request to the Mobile Services Mobile Connectivity feature returned HTTP 400 error response code with message: Validate business configuration of service Mobile Connectivity failed: 400 Bad Request: "The endpoint address is invalid", The endpoint address is invalid"

Does anybody know would here could beeing missed?

The application is successfully created in the Mobile Services, BUT only the app without any settings --> no Connectivity, no Offline-Config,...

Regards

Björn

entry.title: Is anyone using AES/QES e-signature type with RCM/ONB (standard DocuSign Integration)
entry.content:
We are in the process of evaluating e-Signature options for our Global RCM/ONB Implementation. We currently use DocuSign Simple Electronic Signature (SES) with RCM for offer and click to sign in ONB 1.0 (US Only).

We want to use the same e-signature functionality across both modules and have heard from discussions so far that AES/QES  e-signature types is more acceptable in EU and UK and considered to be equivalent of a wet signature than just a Simple Electronic Signature (SE) considering it requires a higher level of identity authentication (digital certificate issue by TSP or face to face id verification).

Has anyone implemented AES/QES via the standard DocuSign integration that we have with RCM and ONB 2.0 and can share their experience. I understand the verification part would be in DocuSign but wanted to know more about whether the redirect between both systems (SF & DoucSign) is seamless irrespective of the type of e-signature option you use with the standard DocSign integration and what is the experience of the candidates that have used it. 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2406
entry.content:
We're excited to share the latest news around SAP Asset Performance Management (APM). This blog explores transformative features including AI features such as anomaly detection for enhanced reliability, proactive maintenance planning integration with SAP S/4HANA, enriched asset performance analysis in SAP Analytics Cloud, and improved clarity through terminology updates in several APM applications. Let's explore how these updates empower reliability engineers and asset managers to optimize operations and maximize asset lifespan. 

*for simplicity I may refer in the following blog to SAP Asset Performance Management as APM. The blog topics are structured by APM business capability.

 

Asset Health Monitoring

Discover the Power of Anomaly Detection in SAP Asset Performance Management

Ensuring operational continuity and minimizing downtime are critical factors in industrial asset management. However, many face the challenge of a resource crunch when it comes to implementing large-scale condition-based maintenance. 

But here's the good news - We are now introducing Anomaly Detection to SAP Asset Performance Management. This revolutionary feature will significantly change the way reliability engineers and business users harness the power of advanced AI technologies in their everyday work processes.

Our solution, which is designed with Reliability Engineers in mind, is a scalable and user-friendly tool that offers accurate asset alerts. It aids in the formulation of preemptive maintenance strategies, highlighting potential issues before they escalate. This in turn, changes raw data into actionable intelligence, which enables the optimal scheduling of maintenance.

The benefits are manifold: enhanced reliability and lifespan of the asset, effective maintenance cost management, boosted maintenance productivity, and a reduction in downtime.

Watch the video to see how to configure and use anomaly detection in this first release:

 

Asset Reliability Engineering 

Create Maintenance Plans for Proactive-Type Recommendations Directly in SAP Asset Performance Management

Closed-loop integration between asset strategy and maintenance management has taken another step forward! You can now create maintenance plans for proactive-type recommendations that have assigned task lists directly in SAP Asset Performance Management in the Implement Recommendations app (proactive recommendation should be of subtype Calender or Performance). The maintenance plans are synced to SAP S/4HANA.

You can check the progress of the creation in the Job Manager application. If required you can choose Stop to abort the creation of maintenance plan. This is useful when there are multiple technical objects associated with the creation.

You can also manage the statuses of recommendations based on the master data management processes in maintenance management for SAP S/4HANA.

Watch the video:

For more information and a step-by-step guide, see Creating a Maintenance Plan for Recommendation. 

 

Asset Performance Analysis

Enhance Strategy Insights with Integrated Assessment and Recommendation Data in SAP Analytics Cloud

With the latest 2406 feature in SAP Analytics Cloud, you can now use Risk and Criticality (RCM) Assessments, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Assessments, and Strategy Assessments for Classes data, linked seamlessly with recommendation data. This upgrade solves previous challenges reported by users regarding the inability to associate assessment data with recommendation data. Now, reliability engineers and analytics administrators can conveniently visualize and analyze both assessment and recommendation data within a single dashboard. For instance, a user can now observe the assessment details, along with the count of recommendations that are part of each individual assessment.

Users can also bring in any relevant data from the recommendation side as required, enhancing the scope of information at their disposal. 

Asset Risk Assessment

Risk & Criticality Assessment - Visibility of Action 

A new field Action is introduced in the Assignment section for different technical objects that are assigned to a risk & criticality assessment. This new field is a UX improvement to give better visibility of technical objects with actions recommended in one view. For more information on the actions, see Assessing Asset Criticality Based on the Risk Score.

Cross-Product Capabilities

Change reference of Maintainable Items to Object Parts in Assign Maintainable Items dialog

In the Assign Maintainable Items dialog, the reference of 'Maintainable Items' has been changed to 'Object Parts' in the following apps to enhance clarity and alignment with SAP S/4HANA:

  • Manage Strategy Assessment for Classes
  • RCM (Reliability-Centered Maintenance) Assessments
  • FMEA (Failure Modes and Effects Analysis) Assessments 

Explanation: Customers define asset hierarchy levels using entities like FL, Equipment, and Object Parts. "Object Parts" now accurately describes detailed asset components (e.g., a pump’s rotor). "Maintainable Item" remains a generic term for any maintainable entity. The screen title "Assign Maintainable Items" stays unchanged, but code sections now specify "Object Parts." This update also sets the stage for future flexibility in terminology.

Example in Manage Strategy Assessment for Classes app:

Keep in touch

Here's a quick reminder how to keep in touch with all things APM:

  • Register right now for our upcoming webcast Supercharge Your Condition-Based Maintenance with IoT and AI using SAP Asset Performance Management on July 24 (10am EDT, 4pm CET) 
  • Check out all past and present Intelligent Asset Management webcasts here. If you register for an IAM webcast, you will be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter. 
  • Check some of our recent blogs:
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2405 
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2404
    • SAP Asset Performance Management Embedding Cumulocity IoT to Drive Innovations in IoT and AI
    • How AI-based Anomaly Detection empowers reliability engineers in SAP Asset Performance Management
    • SAP Asset Performance Management – FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis)
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2403
  • Subscribe to What's New on the SAP Help Portal
  • Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session

See you again soon for the 2407 release update!

entry.title: Re: I am using BAPI BAPI_CTRACDOCUMENT_CREATE giving erro...
entry.content: What is the exact returned message (class or id + number)

entry.title: Re: Sap Datasphere Data Flow Series – Script Operator Part 1
entry.content: In the blog it mentions 'Custom Data Validation and Checks', how would you reference another dataset, for example if I want to validate transaction data against master data. How would I access the master data tables. Also, if you find a validation error, how do you raise that error and how would that error message be conveyed to the users? Any ideas appreciated.

entry.title: Hello experts I would like to know how to get goods receipt for a entry sheet in hours and not in AU
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I would like to know how to receive goods receipt for a service entry sheet in hours and not in AU.
In service master I have maintained H as base unit of Measure but still after goods receipt I got the accounting document in AU. Thanks

entry.title: How to split rows of data?
entry.content:
I have data, where one row, show one case, with a date for received, settled and finished:

CaseIDDate receivedDate settledDate finished
112/6/202414/6/202415/6/2024

I would rather have a single date variable like so:

CaseIDReceived/settled/finishedDate
1Received12/6/2024
1Settled14/6/2024
1Finished15/6/2024

Is this possible and if so, how to?

entry.title: Re: how to print handling units in custom physical invent...
entry.content: Not sure if its possible to get data from Physical inventory document as I found there is only stock information (Storage bin name, PN and QTY to be counted). But you may pair it with Stock and Bin tables based on Storage bin name....

entry.title: Re: Senior SuccessFactors Consultant - The Netherlands
5 Things to Know about Coaching
entry.content:
1. What is coaching?

Think of coaching like forming a professional partnership. It is about sparking deep and creative conversations between you and your coach. It's your vehicle to self-discovery, helping you understand your strengths and pinpoint your developmental goals. Your coach acts as a prompt, partnering with you to efficiently build up your skills and behaviors to make more progress towards these goals. It's about lighting a spark to help you bring out your personal and professional best - like teamwork but with a personal growth twist.

2. Who can benefit from coaching?

Coaching isn't just for a select few; it's for anyone with a thirst for learning and a desire to better themselves. Doesn't matter who you are, as long as you're prepared to roll up your sleeves and dive in. So whether you're looking to conquer a tough challenge, supercharge your growth, welcome big change, or simply unlock the 'you' that you never knew existed, coaching is there for you. It offers a made-to-measure experience that nurtures your strengths, helps you grow, and paves the way for you to shine brighter in your journey!

Here are some examples for you : 

  • Career Transition: Anjali, an accomplished professional, is contemplating a career switch but is unsure how to convey her skills effectively to new industries. A career coach can guide her through this transition, helping to highlight her transferable skills and improve her networking tactics.
  • Leadership Improvement: Rohan has recently become a team leader and is finding it tough to lead effectively. An executive coach can aid him in refining his leadership style, enhancing communication, and fortifying his confidence.
  • Public Speaking: Nitin, a dedicated professional, has a fear of public speaking which hinders his ability to effectively present his ideas. A communications coach can help him gain control over his fear, polish his presentation skills, and improve his confidence, enabling him to communicate effectively in front of an audience.

3. How does coaching work?

Coaching kicks off with a chemistry session, which can be done face-to-face or online, to pinpoint the coachee's goals, hurdles, and what they're aiming for. How often and how these sessions happen depends on what the coachee needs and wants, and they'll tackle tasks that are top priority for them.

The core of coaching is all about boosting self-awareness.

This means the coachee gets to understand their strong points, weak spots, what sets them off, what they value, and how they usually act. It's like a reality check to see where they're at now and where they want to go.

In the world of coaching, being clued in about yourself is key to making the changes you want. Knowing your own thoughts, feelings, and actions, and the effect they have on others, can shine a light on what you might want to work on. This can lead to smarter choices, stronger relationships, and better ways of talking to people.

Coaches are big on using self-awareness to help coachees spot things they might not see and look at things from different angles. They've got a bunch of ways to help coachees become more self-aware, like practical tasks and tests that can nudge them to change their behavior to hit their targets.

Coaching's also about focusing on the good stuff, like handling what life throws at you in a positive way, and talking things out constructively. It's about looking at what's working well and what you need to do to get where you want to be, rather than just zeroing in on the problems. It's meant to get the coachee excited and ready to go after their goals. How long a coach and coachee work together really depends on what the person or business needs and what they prefer.

4. How is it different from other offerings?

  • Leading involves steering and inspiring team members towards a shared vision.
  • Managing is about collaborating with team members to complete daily tasks and achieve organizational objectives.
  • Coaching means fostering personal growth and transformation by tapping into an individual's innate capabilities.
  • Mentoring entails imparting knowledge and insights to nurture and advance someone with less experience.
  • Therapy provides clinical mental health support to assist individuals in managing and alleviating symptoms of mental disorders.

5. Who are coaches?

Credentialed coaches are trained and certified professionals recognized by authoritative bodies like the International Coach Federation (ICF). They've reached specific competence levels, follow a code of ethics, and have completed the required education and experience hours, including passing a thorough exam. To keep their credentials, they must stay current with ongoing professional development, aligning with the latest coaching methods and standards.

Accredited coaches have also completed stringent training and certification, evaluated by institutions like the ICF for their coaching skills, ethical conduct, and dedication to continual learning. Accreditation confirms their ability to deliver top-notch, effective coaching, upholding ethical practices and professional standards. In the coaching field, "accredited" and "credentialed" often mean the same thing, signifying a coach's professional acknowledgment.

Take the next significant step in your personal and professional life with a professional coach. Begin your pioneering journey now! Instead of waiting for the perfect moment, create it. Schedule your initial coaching session to discover your full potential.

entry.title: Re: SAP GOS Objects Migration - Upload Part 2
entry.content:
Hi Vivek,
Can we process this in background.
